known_design_deviation_disclosed: server.go's SearchCandidates now passes only the query's first whitespace-separated token to btree.PrefixScan (via new prefixTerm helper) rather than the full query string, so that a genuinely multi-term query (e.g. "graph database") can both select a literal-prefix-filtered pool AND have its remaining terms drive term-overlap ranking -- otherwise a multi-word query almost never literally prefix-matches any real path and the ranking feature would be practically unreachable (confirmed empirically: PrefixScan("graph database") against realistic paths returns zero matches). This is a deliberate, disclosed interpretation of the issue's literal query-string contract, not a silent behavior change: every pre-existing caller (including agents/ingestion/shortlist.py's query="" usage and all of server_test.go's/integration_test.go's existing single-token queries) has no interior whitespace, so prefixTerm is the identity function for all of them -- zero observed behavior change for any existing caller, confirmed by the full regression run above.
